title: Landmark dynamic survival expert instead of plain binary classification
summary: Dynamic survival work shows that updating risk from longitudinal marker history can outperform static landmark Cox-style models, and discrete hazard parameterizations often beat PMF formulations.
rationale: Dynamic survival work shows that updating risk from longitudinal marker history can outperform static landmark Cox-style models, and discrete hazard parameterizations often beat PMF formulations. This task is naturally a 1-step hazard problem conditional on surviving without a pit so far in the stint.
implementation_hint: Build a separate expert on risk sets only: at lap t, use only cars still active in the stint and summarize recent history causally (last 3 lap deltas, slope, latent tire state, position trend, same-lap field pressure). Try either a coarse landmark random survival forest or a discrete logistic-hazard model over short horizons with quantile-based bins; use the 1-lap hazard as the submission feature/expert output.
expected_effect: A different inductive bias from the current tree classifier that may improve borderline cases by modeling conditional event risk rather than unconditional binary labels.
risk: Heavier engineering and slower CV. Too many landmark times or time bins can fragment data and underperform the simpler baseline.
